description: Gold retrieval evaluation set — synthetic clinical/regulatory document corpus

chunks.text: The device is intended for use in adult patients aged 18 and over with confirmed diagnosis of atrial fibrillation. It is indicated for single-use, short-term cardiac monitoring in a clinical setting under physician supervision.

Contraindications include patients with active implantable devices such as pacemakers or implantable cardioverter-defibrillators. The device should not be used on patients with known skin allergies to medical-grade adhesives.

Risk management activities were performed in accordance with ISO 14971:2019. A total of 47 hazardous situations were identified. Residual risks were evaluated and deemed acceptable after implementation of risk control measures.

Software is classified as Class B per IEC 62304. The software development lifecycle follows a V-model process with documented requirements, design, implementation, verification, and validation phases. All software anomalies are tracked in the issue management system.

Cybersecurity risk assessment was conducted per MDCG 2019-16 guidance. Threat modelling identified five high-severity threats related to data transmission. Mitigations include AES-256 encryption for all data at rest and in transit.

Clinical data was gathered from three sources: literature search, clinical investigation results, and post-market clinical follow-up data. A total of 142 peer-reviewed publications were identified and appraised. The clinical evidence supports the safety and performance claims.

Substantial equivalence was demonstrated with the predicate device ModelX-100 (cleared K193421). Both devices share the same intended use, technology, and indications. Performance characteristics are equivalent within clinically accepted margins.

Post-market surveillance data over 24 months showed a complaint rate of 0.3% and zero serious adverse events attributable to the device. Vigilance reporting obligations were met and no field safety corrective actions were required.

The device must be stored at temperatures between -20°C and +50°C and relative humidity not exceeding 85% non-condensing. Shelf life is 24 months from the date of manufacture when stored under specified conditions.

All electrical safety testing was performed to IEC 60601-1 Ed. 3.1 and IEC 60601-1-2 (EMC). The device passed all Type Tests including dielectric strength, leakage current, and electromagnetic compatibility in clinical environments.

Biocompatibility testing was performed per ISO 10993-1. Tests conducted include cytotoxicity, sensitisation, intracutaneous reactivity, and acute systemic toxicity. All results met acceptance criteria with no biological risks identified.

Usability engineering was conducted per IEC 62366-1. Summative usability evaluation involved 15 representative users from the intended population. No use errors resulting in harm were observed during the evaluation.

Are there any contraindications for patients with pacemakers?

queries.query: How was risk management performed and what standard was followed?
What IEC 62304 software class is the device classified under?
What cybersecurity measures protect data transmission?
queries.notes: Cybersecurity — should rank above general risk management

queries.query: What clinical evidence supports the safety and performance claims?
Which predicate device was used to demonstrate substantial equivalence?

What are the storage temperature and shelf life requirements?

What biocompatibility tests were conducted per ISO 10993?
How many users participated in the summative usability evaluation?
What software development lifecycle model is used?
Encryption standard used for data security
Is the device safe for patients with skin allergies?
queries.notes: Indirect contraindication query — requires semantic understanding
